Kumaran, D., Hassabis, D. & McClelland, J.L. (2016). 'What learning systems do intelligent agents need? Complementary learning systems theory updated.' Trends in Cognitive Sciences 20(7): 512-534
atoms.finance_analogy: Maps directly onto 'frozen slow model (neocortex) + recent-experience replay (hippocampus).' The project's own memory note — 'continual learning UNDERPERFORMS frozen' — is a textbook CLS prediction: naive fast online updating causes catastrophic interference, so value requires slow, interleaved consolidation behind a frozen certification gate rather than greedy retraining.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T3 (the bot HAS memory/experience storing the economic world-picture, and enrichment is slow consolidation into schema). Also RETRO-JUSTIFIES the frozen-gate architecture: CLS explains WHY the frozen champion beats continual learning, turning an empirical project finding into a principled one.

atoms.claim: Schema-consistent information is consolidated dramatically FASTER: in Tse et al.'s rodent studies, new facts congruent with a pre-existing schema were assimilated into neocortex within 48 hours (vs weeks normally) and depended on medial prefrontal cortex. Human work shows congruent information is generally better remembered while strongly INCONGRUENT (schema-violating) information recruits a different, salience/novelty-driven pathway.
sources: Tse, D., Langston, R.F., Kakeyama, M., Bethus, I., Spooner, P.A., Wood, E.R., Witter, M.P. & Morris, R.G.M. (2007). 'Schemas and memory consolidation.' Science 316(5821): 76-82
van Kesteren, M.T.R., Ruiter, D.J., Fernandez, G. & Henson, R.N. (2012). 'How schema and novelty augment memory formation.' Trends in Neurosciences 35(4): 211-219
atoms.finance_analogy: The 2-state regime belief IS the schema: data congruent with the current regime is assimilated cheaply (small belief update), while a regime break (schema violation) is the expensive, salient event that should trigger relearning — matching the 10-day regime horizon and the 'arming on new-high / regime-change' research leads. Enrichment should be schema-gated, not uniform over every daily bar.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T3's 'enrichment' claim with a specific mechanism: new experience integrates faster when it fits the world model, and mismatch is what drives structural change. QUALIFIES the naive loop: consolidation is NOT uniform prediction-error minimization — congruent and incongruent errors are routed to different systems, so 'learn from the difference' is conditional on schema fit.

atoms.claim: The cortex is organized as a HIERARCHY OF TIMESCALES: intrinsic fluctuation timescales and 'temporal receptive windows' lengthen systematically from sensory areas (tens of milliseconds) to prefrontal/association cortex (seconds and longer). Friston's theory derives this from a generative model whose higher levels encode slower environmental causes; Murray et al. and Hasson et al. confirm it empirically across species and methods — higher abstraction demonstrably pairs with a longer integration/prediction horizon.
sources: Kiebel, S.J., Daunizeau, J. & Friston, K.J. (2008). 'A hierarchy of time-scales and the brain.' PLoS Computational Biology 4(11): e1000209
Murray, J.D. et al. (2014). 'A hierarchy of intrinsic timescales across primate cortex.' Nature Neuroscience 17(12): 1661-1663
Hasson, U., Yang, E., Vallines, I., Heeger, D.J. & Rubin, N. (2008). 'A hierarchy of temporal receptive windows in human cortex.' Journal of Neuroscience 28(10): 2539-2550
LeCun, Y. (2022). 'A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence (H-JEPA).' OpenReview v0.9.2
atoms.finance_analogy: The project ALREADY instantiates this: price-level DAILY prediction is a triple-confirmed null (fast, high-entropy, like V1), while the only certified value is the REGIME belief at a 10-day horizon (slow, abstract, like prefrontal cortex). The (abstraction level, prediction horizon) pairing isn't an analogy to import — it is the empirical shape of the codebase's own results.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T5 as the single best-grounded thesis in the thread — the 'higher abstraction = longer horizon' claim is a replicated empirical property of cortex, not a metaphor, and LeCun's H-JEPA independently converges on it as an engineering necessity. Directly licenses building the world-model as a stack of increasingly-abstract, increasingly-long-horizon predictors.

atoms.claim: T2's 'predict the REPRESENTATION, not the raw price' aligns with LeCun/JEPA but is a genuine DEPARTURE from classical predictive coding and the free-energy principle, which are GENERATIVE: they reconstruct/predict sensory input, and the error minimized bottoms out at the level of sensations. JEPA deliberately abandons input reconstruction to predict in latent space precisely so it can ignore unpredictable surface detail — a design the brain-theory tradition does not straightforwardly endorse.
sources: LeCun, Y. (2022). 'A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence.' OpenReview v0.9.2
Assran, M. et al. (2023). 'Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (I-JEPA).' CVPR 2023
Rao, R.P.N. & Ballard, D.H. (1999). Nature Neuroscience 2(1): 79-87 (generative/reconstructive contrast)
Friston, K. (2010). Nature Reviews Neuroscience 11(2): 127-138 (sensory-prediction objective)
atoms.finance_analogy: This is WHY predicting raw daily returns is a triple-null while predicting the abstract regime representation certifies: the price level is the 'unpredictable pixel detail' JEPA throws away, and the regime is the structural latent worth predicting. But note the honest seam — the brain (per predictive coding) would still try to reconstruct the tick, whereas the project (correctly, per JEPA) refuses to.
atoms.relevance: QUALIFIES T2/T6: the 'predict a representation' move is better justified by machine-learning engineering (LeCun) than by cognitive science, which mostly predicts sensations. The human-likeness is partial — brains and JEPA disagree on WHAT is predicted — so T2 should cite JEPA as its warrant, not the FEP.

atoms.claim: The STRONG framing 'this is how humans learn and how science works' rests on the free-energy principle, which leading critics argue is unfalsifiable and explanatorily hollow as a grand unifying theory, and on prediction-error/prediction NEURONS whose dedicated existence has not been cleanly demonstrated. A sober review — co-authored by predictive-processing champion Andy Clark — concludes the key architectural prediction (separate error vs prediction units) lacks decisive evidence; and 'dark room' arguments show pure surprise-minimization does not by itself explain why agents seek stimulation.
sources: Sanchez-Canizares, J. (2021). 'The Free Energy Principle: Good Science and Questionable Philosophy in a Grand Unifying Theory.' Entropy 23(2): 238
Colombo, M. & Wright, C. (2021). 'First principles in the life sciences: the free-energy principle, organicism, and mechanism.' Synthese 198(Suppl 14): 3463-3488
Bowers, J.S. & Davis, C.J. (2012). 'Bayesian just-so stories in psychology and neuroscience.' Psychological Bulletin 138(3): 389-414

Walsh, K.S., McGovern, D.P., Clark, A. & O'Connell, R.G. (2020). 'Evaluating the neurophysiological evidence for predictive processing as a model of perception.' Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences 1464(1): 242-268
Sun, Z. & Firestone, C. (2020). 'The Dark Room Problem.' Trends in Cognitive Sciences 24(5): 346-348
atoms.finance_analogy: Treat T6/human-likeness as a DESIGN HEURISTIC that supplies architectural priors (hierarchy, prediction-error updating, dual memory), NOT as an empirical warranty — the same way a factor model is a useful lens, not a law of markets. Selling the bot as 'it learns the way humans and science do' is a Bayesian just-so story; the defensible claim is 'it is BUILT on mechanisms for which there is partial neural evidence.'
atoms.relevance: CONTRADICTS the literal reading of T1/T3/T6 ('this is how humans learn and how science works'). The falsification-culture takeaway: the individual bricks (predictive coding, RPE, CLS, timescale hierarchy) are well-evidenced, but the CATHEDRAL (FEP as one law unifying it all, implemented by error neurons) is not — so the theory document should assert the bricks and flag the cathedral as a motivating conjecture, not a result.

threads.thread: JEPA-style / predictive-in-latent SSL for time series and finance (2023-2026). What exists: TS-JEPA (NeurIPS-24 workshop), LaT-PFN (JEPA+PFN zero-shot), MTS-JEPA (codebook regimes, early warning), LeJEPA/SIGReg (provable anti-collapse), V-JEPA 2 (latent world model + planning, vision), and exactly one direct finance JEPA (Fin-JEPA, SSRN 2026). What worked: latent-space prediction as pretext is competitive on generic TS benchmarks and captures discrete regime structure; collapse is now solved in a principled way. What did NOT work: JEPA prediction-error as a daily equity trading signal (AUC 0.49), off-the-shelf TS foundation-model transfer to finance, and aggressive test-time adaptation on real markets. Untested: EBM proper in finance, JEPA at the multi-asset regime level (exactly the CRYSTAL-WORLD slot: the field validated the representation layer but has not yet monetized or regime-certified it, whereas this project has a certified regime rule but no learned representation layer - the complementarity is the research opening).

atoms.claim: Adapting JEPA to time series (patch tokens, high mask ratios >70%, prediction performed in latent space rather than input space) matches or surpasses masked-input and contrastive SSL baselines on standard TS classification AND forecasting benchmarks, with claimed robustness to noise and confounding variables that input-space reconstruction lacks.
sources: Joint Embeddings Go Temporal (TS-JEPA), Sofiane Ennadir, Siavash Golkar, Leopoldo Sarra, NeurIPS 2024 Workshop on Time Series in the Age of Large Models, arXiv:2509.25449
atoms.finance_analogy: The pretext task becomes predicting next month's abstract market-state summary (the analyst's one-line read: risk-off rotation, breadth narrowing) instead of the raw price path - and the summary, unlike the path, is not contaminated by microstructure noise. For the daily Dow panel this argues the SSL target should be the future embedding of the 41-col feature block, not future returns.
atoms.relevance: Directly supports T2 (predict the representation, not the raw price) - the core TS prior art for a CRYSTAL-WORLD model. Caveat that bounds the support: single workshop paper, generic UCR/ETT-style benchmarks, zero finance data; the strength is inherited from the vision lineage (I-JEPA/V-JEPA), not from TS-native replication.

atoms.claim: The first direct JEPA on equities (Fin-JEPA: 367K params, SIGReg anti-collapse, 6,230 US stocks 2010-2026, 2.07M samples) proves daily equity panels contain learnable latent temporal structure (latent prediction beats an identity baseline by 10.1%, MSE 0.2922 vs 0.3249) - yet the prediction error used as a trading signal across 300 stocks / 393K windows is weak-to-negligible (AUC ~0.49, Spearman r ~ 0).
sources: Fin-JEPA: Joint-Embedding Predictive Representation Learning for Financial Time Series, Yihan Wang, SSRN working paper 6855118, 2026 (code: github.com/cedricwyh/fin-jepa)
atoms.finance_analogy: An EMH echo: you can compress the market's state (volatility clusters, factor comovement are real, learnable structure) but the compressible part is already priced - like knowing GARCH structure exists without being able to monetize direction from it. Independent external replication of this project's own triple-confirmed daily price-level VoI~0 null, now at the representation level.
atoms.relevance: QUALIFIES T1/T2 sharply: latent predictability is NOT tradability - JEPA succeeding as world-picture compression (T1's actual stated goal) while failing as an alpha source. Simultaneously it is the strongest external validation that the project's daily-price null is a property of the substrate, not of its models; any CRYSTAL-WORLD build must aim its VoI claim at a non-price target (regime, risk) from day one.

atoms.claim: LaT-PFN shows that combining a JEPA latent (prediction-optimized representation of the generating stochastic process) with a prior-data-fitted network and a NORMALIZED ABSTRACT TIME AXIS yields zero-shot in-context forecasting that beats established baselines at any time granularity and horizon, with emergent discrete patch tokens encoding local structures without explicit training.
sources: LaT-PFN: A Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture for In-context Time-series Forecasting, Stijn Verdenius, Andrea Zerio, Roy L.M. Wang, arXiv:2405.10093, 2024
atoms.finance_analogy: Business-cycle time vs calendar time: recessions are one template stretched over 6-30 months, so a model on an abstract time axis can transfer one learned cycle shape across fast (2020) and slow (2008) instances - the formalization of what macro analysts do when they say 'we are in the 1946 analog, sped up'.
atoms.relevance: Supports T5 (abstraction extends the usable horizon) via a mechanism the lecture did not name: abstracting the TIME AXIS itself, not just the state. Also supports T2. The emergent discrete tokens are an unplanned echo of MTS-JEPA's codebook and of this project's 6-symbol alphabet - three independent routes converging on discrete codes over market latents.

atoms.claim: MTS-JEPA (multi-resolution JEPA with a soft codebook bottleneck) explicitly decouples transient shocks from long-term trends and uses the discrete codebook to capture REGIME TRANSITIONS, achieving state-of-the-art time-series anomaly EARLY-WARNING (precursor detection before the event) while the codebook doubles as anti-collapse regularization.
sources: MTS-JEPA: Multi-Resolution Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for Time-Series Anomaly Prediction, Yanan He, Yunshi Wen, Xin Wang, Tengfei Ma, arXiv:2602.04643, 2026
atoms.finance_analogy: A learned, richer regime alphabet over continuous market features - exactly the generalization of this project's certified 2-state P(bear) belief (a hand-built codebook of size 2). Early-warning-before-anomaly is the drawdown-precursor problem: the certified rule fires ON regime evidence; a codebook JEPA is the literature's candidate mechanism for firing BEFORE it.
atoms.relevance: Supports T3 and T5 together: the discrete code (higher abstraction) is precisely what buys prediction-ahead-of-the-event (longer effective horizon). This is the closest architectural blueprint in the literature to a CRYSTAL-WORLD regime layer - but it is a February 2026 preprint, unreplicated, benchmarked on industrial anomaly suites, not markets.

atoms.claim: LeJEPA proves isotropic Gaussian embeddings uniquely minimize worst-case downstream risk and delivers SIGReg, a linear-time regularizer (1D-projection goodness-of-fit tests) that eliminates representation collapse BY DESIGN - removing the EMA teachers, stop-gradients, and schedulers that previously made JEPAs fragile and data-hungry; meanwhile energy-based models proper remain essentially untested in finance (nearest neighbor: ScoreGrad's continuous-EBM probabilistic TS forecaster, on non-financial benchmarks).
sources: LeJEPA: Provable and Scalable Self-Supervised Learning Without the Heuristics, Randall Balestriero, Yann LeCun, arXiv:2511.08544, 2025
ScoreGrad: Multivariate Probabilistic Time Series Forecasting with Continuous Energy-based Generative Models, Tijin Yan et al., arXiv:2106.10121, 2021
atoms.finance_analogy: Replacing an ad-hoc patchwork of risk limits with a single coherent capital rule: one hyperparameter, provable no-collapse. Practically, this is what makes a SMALL-DATA finance JEPA trainable at all - Fin-JEPA's 367K-param model already trains stably with SIGReg on ~2M daily samples, a regime where heuristic-era JEPAs (built for vision-scale data) would collapse.
atoms.relevance: Supports T4 ('clever compare' is now principled, not folklore) and de-risks any T2 experiment on this project's small daily panels. Negative knowledge for T4's stronger reading: no published EBM inference-by-energy-minimization result exists in finance (2023-2026) - that limb of the lecture is an open, untested bet, not prior art.

atoms.claim: V-JEPA 2 (1M+ hours of video, masked prediction in latent space) delivers state-of-the-art physical-world understanding and ZERO-SHOT robot manipulation via model-predictive control executed entirely in the learned embedding space - but long-horizon tasks succeed (65-80%) only when explicitly decomposed into visual SUBGOALS; horizon does not extend for free from the abstraction alone.
sources: V-JEPA 2: Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning, Mido Assran et al. (Meta AI), arXiv:2506.09985, 2025
atoms.finance_analogy: Portfolio planning as MPC over a learned market-state representation instead of price scenario trees; the subgoal finding maps to glide-path investing - a long-horizon allocation plan only works when decomposed into intermediate abstract milestones ('be defensive before recession confirmation', 'redeploy on regime exit'), not as one long rollout of the world model.
atoms.relevance: Strongest established support for T1/T3 (a latent world model that closes the analyze-predict-act-compare loop and acts through it) and for T6 (perception-to-planning like humans). CRITICALLY QUALIFIES T5: even at Meta scale, longer horizons required hand-engineered hierarchical subgoals - the 'higher abstraction = longer horizon' property had to be built in, it did not emerge. Expect the same on markets.

atoms.claim: On real financial series both halves of the naive transfer story fail: off-the-shelf time-series foundation models underperform in zero-shot AND after fine-tuning (only finance-native pretraining yields forecasting and economic gains - first comprehensive TSFM-in-finance study), and aggressive test-time adaptation of a pretrained forecaster DEGRADES performance on SPY/QQQ/EURUSD across pandemic-inflation-recovery regimes, where a frozen backbone with batch-norm-statistics-only updates is the robust default.
sources: Re(Visiting) Time Series Foundation Models in Finance, Eghbal Rahimikia, Hao Ni, Weiguan Wang, arXiv:2511.18578, 2025
Test-Time Adaptation for Non-stationary Time Series: From Synthetic Regime Shifts to Financial Markets, Yurui Wu, Qingying Deng, Wonou Chung, Mairui Li, arXiv:2602.00073, 2026
FinCast: A Foundation Model for Financial Time-Series Forecasting, arXiv:2508.19609, 2025 (consistent)
atoms.finance_analogy: Hiring a brilliant physicist to trade: general pattern skill without market-native priors loses to a modestly-sized finance-pretrained model. The adaptation half is this project's own E-15/CL result rediscovered externally: the frozen certified rule beat continual learning, and here frozen-plus-minimal-renormalization beats every aggressive online update on real markets.
atoms.relevance: QUALIFIES T2/T6 (representation skill is not domain-portable; a CRYSTAL-WORLD model must pretrain on market data, not generic TS corpora) and QUALIFIES T3: the lecture's 'learn from the predicted-vs-actual difference' loop, implemented naively as continuous weight updates, is harmful under market nonstationarity - the difference signal must flow to a slow, gated, structural level (rules through a frozen certification gate), which is exactly the architecture this project already runs.

atoms.claim: The TS SSL literature shows a stable division of labor: contrastive pretraining (TS2Vec-family) captures high-level instance semantics and wins at classification but underperforms on fine-grained low-level forecasting, while masked/reconstructive modeling wins the low-level tasks (SimMTM, NeurIPS 2023, its manifold-based multi-mask formulation beating TS2Vec/CoST/TF-C and Ti-MAE/TST, with the smallest representation gap: CKA difference 1.15% vs 13.75% for TS2Vec).
sources: SimMTM: A Simple Pre-Training Framework for Masked Time-Series Modeling, Jiaxiang Dong, Haixu Wu, Haoran Zhang, Li Zhang, Jianmin Wang, Mingsheng Long, NeurIPS 2023, arXiv:2302.00861
TS2Vec: Towards Universal Representation of Time Series, Zhihan Yue et al., AAAI 2022
atoms.finance_analogy: Regime calls vs price fills: classifying which market you are in (bull/bear, risk-on/off) is a high-level-semantics task where contrastive-style representations suffice, while filling in tomorrow's curve is the low-level task - and this project's own ledger (certified value ONLY at the 10d regime-classification level, price-level daily forecasting a confirmed null) says the market pays for exactly the task family that coarse representations serve.
atoms.relevance: Supports T5 with the field's own task taxonomy: the pretext-paradigm-to-task mapping IS the abstraction-level-to-horizon mapping (coarse/semantic representations serve long-horizon regime questions; fine-grained ones serve short-horizon curve-filling, which in markets has no value). JEPA's pitch - latent prediction spanning both niches - is what TS-JEPA claims and what remains UNTESTED on financial panels; benchmarks here are UCR/ETT-style, not markets.

threads.thread: PRIMARY SOURCES on LeCun's program. Verified against: (a) Dawid & LeCun "Introduction to Latent Variable EBMs" (arXiv:2306.02572, J.Stat.Mech 2024) — full text extracted and read verbatim (LeCun-coauthored writeup of the same Les Houches 2022 lecture material as the position paper, incl. Sections 3-4 on EBMs/collapse and 6.2 on H-JEPA); (b) I-JEPA CVPR 2023 abstract/mechanism; (c) V-JEPA (arXiv:2404.08471) full-text quantitative tables; (d) V-JEPA 2 (arXiv:2506.09985) full HTML incl. limitations section; (e) Harvard lecture identified as the 2024 Ding Shum Lecture "Objective-Driven AI" (Harvard CMSA, 2024-03-28; the CMSA YouTube video = Ivan's youtu.be/yUmDRxV0krg); (f) counter-sources: arXiv:2505.24187 "Beyond Exponential Decay", LessWrong "Contra LeCun"; (g) finance transfer: Fin-JEPA (SSRN 2026), LaT-PFN, TS-JEPA. The OpenReview PDF of the 2022 position paper itself is behind a bot-check; all position-paper claims below are grounded in the LeCun-coauthored lecture-notes companion, which is faithful and citable. Net read for the theory doc: the representation-prediction and hierarchy-horizon pillars (T2, T5) are well-grounded and the project's own regime-vs-price results are arguably better evidence for T5 than anything in the JEPA corpus; the anti-generative rhetoric (feeding T2's framing) is the weakest pillar and should be stated in its defensible efficiency form; the enrichment loop (T3) is a training-time story in every deployed JEPA system, not yet an online act-and-learn story; and Fin-JEPA independently reproduces the project's central null (representations learnable, direct alpha absent).

atoms.claim: Predicting in learned representation space beats generative pixel reconstruction for learning semantic structure because the encoder is free to discard unpredictable, irrelevant detail: V-JEPA states latent-space prediction has 'the flexibility to eliminate irrelevant or unpredictable pixel-level details from the target representation', and under frozen evaluation beats pixel-reconstruction VideoMAE by +4.0pp (SSv2 69.5 vs 65.5) and +3.0pp (K400 80.8 vs 77.8) with ~2x pretraining speedup and fewer samples (270M vs 410M); I-JEPA learns 'highly semantic' features without augmentations, training ViT-H/14 in <72h on 16 A100s.
sources: Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (I-JEPA) — Assran, Duval, Misra, Bojanowski, Vincent, Rabbat, LeCun, Ballas; CVPR 2023 (arXiv:2301.08243)
Revisiting Feature Prediction for Learning Visual Representations from Video (V-JEPA) — Bardes, Garrido, Ponce, Chen, Xu, Rabbat, LeCun, Assran, Ballas; arXiv:2404.08471 / TMLR, 2024
A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence v0.9.2 — LeCun; OpenReview, 2022
atoms.finance_analogy: Do not forecast tomorrow's price vector (dominated by unpredictable microstructure noise, the market's 'blades of grass'); forecast the next market-STATE representation. The project's certified P(bear) regime belief is exactly a low-dim predicted representation, and its 10d risk rule works while raw daily price prediction is a triple-confirmed null — the encoder 'discarded' the unpredictable component.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T2 directly and T1 (the representation IS the world picture). Qualifier: the win is proven under frozen-eval in vision/video; with full fine-tuning pixel models close much of the gap, so the honest claim is sample/compute-efficiency and semantic abstraction, not universal dominance.

atoms.claim: Energy-based models replace normalized probability with a scalar compatibility score: F(x,y) 'capture[s] dependencies between variables x and y by associating a scalar energy to each configuration', inference is y* = argmin_y F(x,y), which (i) natively represents multimodal futures (several y's can share low energy), (ii) frees the loss/scoring choice from likelihood, and (iii) avoids the intractable partition function of continuous high-dim probabilistic models — at the stated price that 'energies are uncalibrated... so combining two separately trained EBMs is not straightforward' and uncertainty is harder to read off.
sources: A Tutorial on Energy-Based Learning — LeCun, Chopra, Hadsell, Ranzato, Huang; in Predicting Structured Data, MIT Press, 2006
Introduction to Latent Variable Energy-Based Models: A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence — Dawid, LeCun; arXiv:2306.02572, J. Stat. Mech., 2024 (Sections 3-3.2, quotes verbatim)
atoms.finance_analogy: This is relative-value ranking versus density forecasting: a desk deciding which asset to hold needs the correct ORDERING of scores under current conditions (the source's own chess example: you need the best move, not calibrated move probabilities). Caveat maps too: uncalibrated energies across models = you cannot naively combine two separately-fit signal scores, the same reason the project forces exposure-matched twins rather than comparing raw statistics across differently-scaled rules.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T4 ('clever compare representations' = D(sx,sy) is the energy; low energy = compatible states). Qualifies T4 with the honest costs LeCun himself lists: no calibrated uncertainty, no cross-model composability without extra design.

atoms.claim: Joint-embedding architectures have a degenerate failure mode — collapse — where 'the two encoders are likely to ignore inputs entirely and just produce identical constant outputs'; every model capable of multimodal prediction is susceptible. Two remedy families: contrastive methods (push up energy of generated negatives), which suffer 'bad (even exponential!) scaling... with the data dimension', vs regularized/architectural methods that bound the low-energy volume — VICReg's variance floor + covariance decorrelation + invariance terms, or the asymmetric EMA-target-encoder/stop-gradient route used by BYOL, I-JEPA and V-JEPA. LeCun's program bets on the regularized family.
sources: VICReg: Variance-Invariance-Covariance Regularization for Self-Supervised Learning — Bardes, Ponce, LeCun; ICLR 2022 (arXiv:2105.04906)
Introduction to Latent Variable Energy-Based Models — Dawid, LeCun; arXiv:2306.02572, 2023/2024 (Sections 4-4.2, 6.1, quotes verbatim)
Bootstrap Your Own Latent (BYOL) — Grill et al.; NeurIPS 2020
atoms.finance_analogy: The project has already met collapse's RL cousin twice: PPO heads converging to CONSTANT exposure dials (E-27/28), and the degenerate z_dsd statistic that certified everything until dial-twins were mandated. A trading world model trained to predict its own next state will happily learn 'the state is always the same' — VICReg-style variance floors on the belief (does P(bear) actually vary? are belief components decorrelated?) are the direct analogue of the project's exposure-matched-twin rule.
atoms.relevance: QUALIFIES T2/T4 critically: compare-representations learning fails SILENTLY without explicit anti-collapse machinery — a mandatory falsifier for any JEPA-style bot component. Note the contrastive-scaling claim is contested at scale (CLIP is contrastive and works); tag that sub-claim Plausible, not Established.

atoms.claim: H-JEPA's hierarchy-horizon principle: stacked JEPAs where 'a lower-level JEPA-1 is used for short-time predictions based on many details, and a higher-level JEPA-2 makes predictions for the longer term based on fewer details', because detail that enables precise short-term prediction 'may not be needed or be even hurtful for long-term predictions'. Crucially this is still a PROPOSAL: no full H-JEPA has been demonstrated; V-JEPA 2 (the program's flagship) explicitly lists hierarchical planning as future work and needs hand-specified sub-goals for long-horizon tasks, with representation-space rollouts degrading via error accumulation.
sources: A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence — LeCun; OpenReview, 2022 (Section 4.6)
Introduction to Latent Variable Energy-Based Models — Dawid, LeCun; arXiv:2306.02572, 2024 (Section 6.2, quotes verbatim)
V-JEPA 2: Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning — Assran et al.; arXiv:2506.09985, 2025 (limitations)
atoms.finance_analogy: The project's headline result IS this principle instantiated: daily price-level prediction = VoI~0 (the 'quantum' level, seconds-horizon in Ivan's T5 phrasing), while the 2-state regime abstraction certifies at H=10d (the 'thermodynamic' level) — arguably stronger evidence for the hierarchy-horizon law on markets than anything in the JEPA corpus, since LeCun's version was never built end-to-end. Econometric cousin: aggregation — Sharpe/vol estimates stabilize at longer horizons while daily direction stays a coin flip.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T5 (its central claim, near-verbatim from the primary source) but downgrades it from 'established architecture' to 'principle with convergent evidence, unproven engineering'. Also supports T1: the certified value living only at the regime level says the WORLD PICTURE, not the price path, is the object worth predicting.

atoms.claim: V-JEPA 2-AC demonstrates the plan-by-comparing-representations loop at deployment scale: pretrain action-free on >1M hours of video, post-train a latent ACTION-CONDITIONED predictor on only 62h of unlabeled robot data, then plan via model-predictive control that 'minimize[s] the L1 distance between the world model's imagined future state representations and its goal representation' (CEM, receding horizon) — zero-shot pick-and-place on Franka arms in unseen labs, ~16 s/action vs ~4 min for the pixel-generative Cosmos world model (~15x). Acknowledged limits: autoregressive representation rollouts accumulate error, camera-pose sensitivity, goals must be given as images.
sources: V-JEPA 2: Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning — Assran, Bardes, Rabbat, LeCun, Ballas et al. (Meta); arXiv:2506.09985, 2025
atoms.finance_analogy: Portfolio control as MPC in belief space: predict the next market-state representation under each candidate exposure and pick the action minimizing distance to a goal state (e.g., a DD-budget-respecting state) — structurally what the certified rule does each 10d step. The error-accumulation limit is the direct analogue of why the project rebalances on short certified horizons instead of trusting long model rollouts; the 15x speedup is the argument for latent-space (not scenario-price-path) planning.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T3's analyze->predict-own-representation->act->compare loop and T1 — with one honest qualification: in every deployed JEPA system the predicted-vs-actual comparison happens at TRAINING time; online enrichment of the world model from the agent's own live actions (T3's full loop, and the HL loop's ambition) is not yet demonstrated anywhere in this literature.

atoms.claim: LeCun's Harvard-lecture argument that autoregressive LLMs are doomed by exponential error divergence — P(correct) ~ (1-e)^n per token, hence 'Auto-Regressive LLMs fall short' as a path to human-level AI — is REJECTED in its strong form: transformers are not Markov chains (attention re-reads the entire generated history, so per-token errors are neither independent nor irrecoverable), correctness is not binary, models can self-correct within context, and empirically LLMs sustain coherence over thousands of tokens. The defensible residue of his claim is only the efficiency/grounding argument for continuous high-dim signals, not the divergence theorem.
sources: Objective-Driven AI: Towards AI systems that can learn, remember, reason, and plan — LeCun; Ding Shum Lecture, Harvard CMSA, 2024-03-28 (the youtu.be/yUmDRxV0krg lecture)
Beyond Exponential Decay: Rethinking Error Accumulation in Large Language Models — arXiv:2505.24187, 2025
Contra LeCun on 'Autoregressive LLMs are doomed' — LessWrong, 2023
atoms.finance_analogy: Iterated one-step forecasts do compound errors (classic ARMA multi-step degradation), but DIRECT forecasting at the target horizon sidesteps iteration — exactly the project's choice of a native 10d rule over chaining ten 1d forecasts. Also a governance lesson: the project's own improvement loop is LLM-DRIVEN (Heuristic Learning), which works precisely because LeCun's doom argument fails in practice.
atoms.relevance: CONTRADICTS the anti-generative framing bundled into T2: the theory doc should claim 'representation prediction is more sample/compute-efficient and better abstracted for continuous market signals', NOT 'token/price-level generative prediction is mathematically broken'. Ivan's T2 survives; the lecture's strongest rhetorical support for it does not.

atoms.claim: LeCun's data-efficiency argument for world models: a 4-year-old has taken in more sensory data (~1E15 bytes through vision, lecture figure) than the largest LLMs see in text (~1E13-1E14 bytes), yet humans learn robust common sense from it — where common sense is defined as 'the ability to use models of the world to fill in information about the world that is unavailable from perception or memory (e.g., predict the future)'; SSL on prediction is proposed as the central mechanism because it extracts the most bits of learning signal per sample (vs sparse-reward RL and label-limited SL). The bandwidth arithmetic is solid; the inference 'human-level AI therefore requires sensory-grounded world models' remains conjecture.
sources: Objective-Driven AI — LeCun; Ding Shum Lecture, Harvard CMSA, 2024
Introduction to Latent Variable Energy-Based Models — Dawid, LeCun; arXiv:2306.02572, 2024 (Sections 2.2-2.4 and Conclusion, common-sense quote verbatim)
A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence — LeCun; OpenReview, 2022
atoms.finance_analogy: Markets are the extreme small-effective-sample regime — one non-repeatable macro history — so storing raw paths buys little; the value is in compression into a small reusable state (Ivan's 'science compresses: planetary motion in 6 numbers' = describing a decade of market data by P(bear) + vol regime + dial). The 'fill in what perception can't see' definition of common sense is precisely what a regime belief does on days the tape is ambiguous.
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T3 (learning = compression into a world picture, not raw storage) and partially T6 — but qualifies T6: LeCun's actual argument is about DATA EFFICIENCY, not human-likeness for its own sake; 'maximally similar to humans' is philosophy layered on top, and the counter-example (LLMs succeed while being maximally unhuman in learning style) sits in the same lecture hall.

atoms.claim: JEPA transfers to financial time series but reproduces the alpha null: Fin-JEPA — first JEPA on financial data (6,230 US equities 2010-2026, 2.07M samples, 367K-param PriceEncoder + causal Transformer predictor, SIGReg anti-collapse regularization from LeCun's 2025 LeJEPA line) — trains stably with no collapse and its latent predictions beat an identity baseline by ~10.1%, yet its prediction-error (VoE) signal tested as a trading signal across 300 stocks is 'weak-to-negligible'. Parallel non-finance evidence (LaT-PFN, TS-JEPA, Phys-JEPA) shows latent-space prediction is a viable general time-series representation strategy.
sources: Fin-JEPA: Joint-Embedding Predictive Representation Learning for Financial Time Series — Yihan Wang; SSRN preprint 6855118 + github.com/cedricwyh/fin-jepa, 2026
LaT-PFN: A Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture for In-context Time-series Forecasting — Verdenius et al.; arXiv:2405.10093, 2024
Joint Embeddings Go Temporal (TS-JEPA) — arXiv:2509.25449, 2025
atoms.finance_analogy: This IS the analogy: an independent group ran LeCun's recipe on equities and landed exactly where the project did — market-state representations are learnable and predictable in latent space, but predictability of the representation does not convert into price-level alpha. It's external replication of the project's VoI~0 daily null AND of its resolution (seek value at the regime/risk level, not the price level).
atoms.relevance: SUPPORTS T1/T2 feasibility (JEPA-on-markets is buildable today, collapse controllable with published regularizers) while CONTRADICTING any reading of T1-T2 that expects representation prediction to yield direct trading edge. Single unreviewed preprint — treat as a lead to verify, and as the natural architecture candidate if the bot's world-model layer is ever rebuilt JEPA-style.
